I have a small CLI tool in this workdir: `mini_grep.py`, built on a vendored argument-parsing module `cliparse.py`. Its `--exclude` flag is supposed to accept any number of patterns (it's declared nargs="*"), but it only ever keeps the FIRST pattern and the remaining ones spill over into the positional arguments. Concrete repro (see README.md): `python mini_grep.py --exclude a b c -- hello f1.txt f2.txt` yields exclude=['a'] but should yield exclude=['a','b','c'] with needle='hello'. Any option declared with nargs="*" or nargs="+" shows this same one-value truncation; fixed-count options like nargs=2 are fine. `mini_grep.py` itself looks correct, so the bug is inside `cliparse.py`. Find and fix the root cause in cliparse.py so multi-value options collect all of their values again. Don't change mini_grep.py or work around it in the app.
